obligation_text: The quality management system shall also include: systems and procedures for data management including data acquisition, data collection, data analysis, data labelling, data storage, data filtration, data mining, data aggregation, data retention and any other operation regarding the data; the risk management system; the setting-up, implementation and maintenance of a post-market monitoring system; procedures related to the reporting of a serious incident; the handling of communication with national competent authorities, other relevant authorities, notified bodies, other operators, customers, or other interested parties; and systems and procedures for record keeping of all relevant documentation and information.
scoring_text: Knowledge of comprehensive AI data management systems including full data lifecycle operations, integration with risk management systems, post-market monitoring system design, serious incident reporting procedures, regulatory authority communication protocols, and documentation and record-keeping systems.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of comprehensive AI data management systems including full data lifecycle operations, integration with risk management systems, post-market monitoring system design, serious incident reporting procedures, regulatory authority communication protocols, and documentation and record-keeping systems.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of the regulatory and compliance landscape governing enterprise data and AI, including data protection law, AI-specific regulation, sector-specific obligations, and cross-jurisdictional compliance requirements.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 17(1)(e-j) (obligation) superset of WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-006 is the strongest match (STS: 0.606, cosine: 0.659). Secondary: SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.635), AG-T-002 (AG, cosine: 0.625).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.1/10 (raw 0.606). Shared vocabulary: [ai, data, knowledge, regulatory]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [authority, communication, comprehensive, design, documentation, full] vs WIDAI terms [compliance, cross, enterprise, governing, jurisdictional, landscape] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.865 (P=0.856, R=0.874). Balanced P/R — the 34-word competency implication and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.5%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.659 > STS 0.606 (gap +0.053). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, data, knowledge, regulatory] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.6%. Shared tokens: [ai, data, knowledge, regulatory]. EU AI Act-only: [authority, communication, comprehensive, design, documentation, full]. WIDAI-only: [compliance, cross, enterprise, governing, jurisdictional, landscape].
